content:
error: target file "./scripts/target.json" does not exist

make[2]: *** [rust/Makefile:429: rust/core.o] Error 1
make[1]: *** [/home/baojg/workspace/Rlinux/Makefile:1293: prepare] Error 2
make: *** [Makefile:234: __sub-make] Error 2
Could you please share some more details about your environment and the commands you run?
latest arch linux vm. lastes rust-dev branch. Have config the make menuconfig. now make LLVM=1 or make CC=clang both output same error.
(deleted)

zcat /proc/config.gz > .config
make LLVM=1 menuconfig
make LLVM=1

🕙[ 2023-08-15 21:38:25 ] ❯ make LLVM=1 rustavailable
Rust is available!
🕙[ 2023-08-15 21:39:42 ] ❯ rustc --version
rustc 1.71.1 (eb26296b5 2023-08-03)
@Miguel Ojeda
so you are using rust-dev right? Please do make ARCH=x86_64 LLVM=1, i.e. adding ARCH=x86_64.
(rename topic accordingly)

Some explanation, in rust-dev branch, the ARM64 support patch is queued there, and it does

ifeq($(ARCH), x86_64))
always-$(CONFIG_RUST)                   += target.json
endif

However, even on a 64bit machine, if you just do make LLVM=1, ARCH will be x86 instead of x86_64 (see how SUBARCH is calculated).
thank your explanation. So we should update the doc also ?
If the ARM64 support gets merged (in rust-next or ARM64 maintainers' tree), we definitely need to either 1) fix the doc or 2) fix the patch so that make LLVM=1 works.
It may be a good idea to mention this in the mailing list thread so that they can fix it before applying it. It is also serves as a ping on the patch series.
I'm not sure it's the ARM64 support that needs a fix: Rust support is only availabe for x86_64 right now, so if some one does a make ARCH=x86 LLVM=1, it of course may not work.

Hmm... I am confused. If the ARM64 patches are merged as they are, they break make LLVM=1 because ARCH=x86 due to SUBARCH=x86, right?

i.e. I am worrying about the "default" case (not specifying ARCH in the command-line), rather than specifying an unsupported one.

In other words, I think that if in the patch should test for SRCARCH == x86 and later, if needed, it may test for CONFIG_64BIT (or just for CONFIG_X86_64).
Or target.json should be opt-in unless it's ARM64.
My point is that arguably the "default" case just "works by accident" ;-)
Do you mean without the patch applied?

I mean if we were to play the "blame game", then the ARM64 patch may not be the only one.

Considering if one was to do a cross-compile for x86_64 on x86 (32bit) machine, the .config gets every bits (64bit, Rust support, etc) needed, would a make LLVM=1 work? If so, what does that mean?
Does it mean we support x86 32 bits?
because make LLVM=1 means "compile with the host architecture"
Anyway, I just pass the ball ;-)

I mean if we were to play the "blame game", then the ARM64 patch may not be the only one.

My point was that we/they cannot apply the patches as they are, because they break something that works now and is a common case (regardless of whose fault is) -- so I suggested letting them know just in case they would apply them.

would a make LLVM=1 work? If so, what does that mean?

I don't follow, sorry. If you do make LLVM=1 on x86_32, then ARCH == x86 and then it should use i386_defconfig if I am reading the Makefile correctly (I have not actually run it). We don't support Rust-builds like that, yeah. But I don't understand how that relates to the patch.

Anyway, I just pass the ball ;-)

Thanks!

On cross-compiling: if you do make ARCH=x86_64 LLVM=1, then ARCH == x86_64 (i.e. not x86), so it works without and with the patch, right? So that is fine (even if the patch should not test ARCH like that).
Out of curiosity, is aarch support expected to land in the 6.6 cycle?
It depends on the arm64 maintainers and what they want to do.
I'll ask, wonder if there are any specific blockers
I would suggest anyone interested in the patches to try them and send a Tested-by if they work (taking into account the changes discussed by Boqun in the thread).
Having said that, a v3 may be needed (for the ARCH test and the squash I suggested).

Miguel Ojeda said:

On cross-compiling: if you do make ARCH=x86_64 LLVM=1, then ARCH == x86_64 (i.e. not x86), so it works without and with the patch, right? So that is fine (even if the patch should not test ARCH like that).

I mean you could get a x86_64 config from somewhere, and use that config on a i686 machine and run make LLVM=1.
(but maybe I'm wrong)
